methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
NextSilicon, an AI & Software company at Series B led by Elad Raz, has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Physical Intelligence, Nominal, Skild AI, Figure AI), supplemented by a broader universe including 1 public comp (Palantir) and 8 private peers.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying $380M. Peer-median valuation anchors to the median valuation of the 4-company comp set at $9.8B, with peers ranging from $1.0B (Nominal) to $39.0B (Figure AI). The dispersion reflects stage variance within the peer set—two Series C positions at $14B–$39B, two Series B at $1.0B–$5.6B.

Triangulating these methods yields a low estimate of $323M, a median of $5.1B, and a high of $11.3B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty inherent in the peer-median approach, given the $38B spread across the comp universe and the early-stage nature of the target. Key sensitivities include peer-set composition weighting (particularly Figure AI's outsized $39B valuation) and whether NextSilicon's capabilities and traction align more closely with the $1B–$5.6B cohort or the higher-valued Series C tier. Stage-progression offers a floor but assumes limited incremental value capture beyond capital deployment.
